content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 12, 1986

Osborn Christon
7708 Chaparral Way
Fair Oaks, Ca 95628

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-86-119

Dear Mr. Christon:

Thank you for your letter requesting advice concerning your
duties under the conflict of interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act.1/ In your letter you provided the
following facts.

FACTS

You are the manager of the Field Services Section of the
Child Nutrition and Food Distribution Division of the
California State Department of Education. The Child Nutrition
and Food Distribution Division is responsible for the
management of child nutrition programs in schools, child care
centers, and residential child care institutions in
California. Under the child nutrition programs, eligible
children in these institutions receive free or reduced-cost
nutritionally adequate meals.

The Field Services Section is responsible for compliance
monitoring and program assistance to school food authorities
and administrators. You have regular contact with school
superintendents, business managers, and food service directors
regarding the applicable performance standards, pupil
eligibility criteria, meal requirements, and other technical
aspects of child nutrition programs. These school
superintendents, business managers, and food service directors
work directly with food processors and vendors who provide food

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Osborn Christon
May 12, 1986
Page 2


and services in connection with the child nutrition programs.
The food processors and vendors rarely have contact with
employees in the Child Nutrition and Food Distribution Division.

     You have stated that you intend to pursue, on a limited,
part-time basis, a business enterprise consisting of raising
crawfish in Louisiana and marketing the crawfish in
California. It is possible that, in marketing the crawfish,
you would have business dealings with some of the food
processors and vendors that service school districts, child
care centers, and residential child care institutions in
connection with the child nutrition programs. However, your
business dealings with food processors and vendors would not
concern the child nutrition programs because crawfish are too
expensive to be served in the child nutrition programs.

QUESTION

     Do your proposed business activities present any conflict
of interest problems under the Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION

     Under the facts presented, your proposed business
activities do not present any conflict of interest problems
under the Political Reform Act.

ANALYSIS

     Section 87100 prohibits any public official at any level of
state or local government from making, participating in, or
using his official position to influence a governmental
decision in which the official knows or has reason to know he
has a financial interest. A public official has a financial
interest in a governmental decision if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial
effect, distinguishable from the effect on the public
generally, on the official or a member of his immediate family,
or on any of the following interests:

     (a)  Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth one
thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

     (b)  Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth one
thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

Osborn Christon
May 12, 1986
Page 3


     (c) Any source of income, other than gifts and
other than loans by a commercial lending institution
in the regular course of business on terms available
to the public without regard to official status,
aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more
in value provided to, received by or promised to the
public official within 12 months prior to the time
when the decision is made.

     (d) Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

     (e) Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent
for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating two
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided
to, received by, or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision
is made.

                              Section 87103(a)-(e).

     Once you commence the enterprise of raising and marketing
crawfish, you will have an investment in a business entity.
See Section 82005. In addition, if you have a 10-percent or
greater ownership interest in the business entity, customers of
the business entity, such as food processors and vendors, would
be considered sources of income to you, based on your pro rata
share of the gross receipts from those customers. Section
82030(a). Thus, you would have the potential for a conflict of
interest based on Section 87103(a) and (c). However, the
Political Reform Act does not prohibit you from holding
financial interests which may create a conflict of interest.
Rather, the Act requires you to disqualify yourself from
participating in governmental decisions when a conflict of
interest exists. Therefore, it is necessary to determine
whether, as manager of the Field Services Division, you make or
participate in any decisions which could foreseeably have a
material financial effect on either your proposed crawfish
business or the customers of that business.

     As a general rule, you are required to examine each
decision in which you participate as Manager of the Field
Service Section to ascertain whether or not it is foreseeable
that the decision would have a significant effect on the
revenues or expenses of either your business or the food

Osborn Christon
May 12, 1986
Page 4


processors and vendors with whom you do business.2/ You have
indicated that, as manager of the Field Services Division, you
are involved in compliance monitoring and assistance to school
districts and other institutions participating in the child
nutrition programs. Your duties concern the nutritional aspects
of the child nutrition programs rather than food purchasing
decisions. You have also stated that your crawfish business
would not include marketing crawfish for use in child nutrition
programs. Based on these facts, we conclude that it is
unlikely that you would be faced with decisions which would
require disqualification.

I trust this general advice answers your concerns. If, in
the future, you would like to discuss a specific situation,
please me at (916) 322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Kathryn E. Donovan
Counsel
Legal Division

KED:plh
Enclosure

_________________

2/ The Commission has adopted regulations which contain
monetary guidelines concerning whether the effect of a decision
on a business entity will be considered material. Please refer
to the enclosed copy of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702.2.

FPPC
APR 14 9:05 AM '86

7708 Chaparral Way
Fair Oaks, California 95628
April 8, 1986

Ms Kathryn Dinovo
Staff Attorney, Legal Division
State Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 207
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Ms Dinovo:

Thank you very much for the guidance given on the phone the other day.

Born employed by the California State Department of Education, Child Nutrition and Food Distribution Division. This division is responsible for the management of Child Nutrition programs in Schools, Child Care Centers and Residential Child Care Institutions in California.

My responsibility in the Management of these Child Nutrition programs is the Field Services Section. The field services section is responsible for Compliance monitoring and Program assistance to School Food Authorities and Administrators represented by Superintendents, Business Managers and Food Service directors. It is very easy, that these food processors and Vendors, that work with schools have any contact with the office and in particular my section.

I intend to pursue, on a limited part time basis) the

Marketing of Crawfish in California. The Crawfish will be raised
in the State of Louisiana. It is possible that I might come in
contact with some of the food processors and vendors that
Service School Districts. Because of the Cost of Crawfish, they
will never be served in the called nutritional programs.

I Certify, that at no time will the Department of Education
Office, table, phone and equipment be used in this limited
part time venture.

As we discussed on the phone, please Certify in writing
that the limited part time venture, in no way conflict
with duties and responsibilities with the California state
Department of Education.

If you need any additional information as discussion,
please call me at (916) 323-2997

Sincerely,
Sidney M. Christen

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


April 17, 1986


Osborn Christion
7708 Chaparral Way
Fair Oaks, CA 95628

Re: 86-119

Dear Mr. Christion:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act has been received by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice
request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or unless more information is needed to answer your request,
you should expect a response within 21 working days.

Very truly yours,

[illegible]

Kathryn E. Donovan
Counsel
Legal Division

KED:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

A public official has a financial 
interest in a governmental decision if it is reasonably 
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial 
effect, distinguishable from the effect on the public 
generally, on the official or a member of his immediate family,
